content.problemStatement:
"Right smart bird you got there, Mr. Potter. She arrived here just five minutes before yourself."**- Tom

**


Hedwig is not only a smart owl, but she is charming as well. Haron likes her very much and has asked her out. Alas, Hedwig is too busy delivering mails and needs Haron to wait for some time. But, she knows Haron would get really bored alone. Hence, she has asked you to play with him. As soon as Haron knew you love competitive coding, he asks you one of his unsolved problems. You now have to solve this problem and tell Haron the solution which will take some time so that Hedwig can come back and go on a date with him.


Haron's problem states : You are given a tree rooted in vertex **1** having **N** vertices. Each vertex has a color associated with it. 


For every vertex **v** you are given an integer which represents its lucky number, let it be **X**. Now you have to find number of distinct colors which occur **exactly** **X** times in subtree of **v**.


**Note : ** Subtree of **v** contains **v** too.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of input contains one integer N denoting the number of vertices in the tree.

The second line contains N space-separated integers where the ith integer is colori, the color of the ith vertex.

The third line contains N space-separated integers where the ith integer is lucky_numberi which denotes the lucky number of the ith vertex.

Next N-1 lines contain two space-separated integers x and y each, denoting that there is an undirected edge between vertices x and y.
content.outputFormat: Print N space-separated integers in one line where the ith integer denotes the required answer for the ith vertex.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
In subtree of 1 colors 2 and 3 occur exactly 2 times.
In subtree of 2 only color 6 appears exactly 1 time.
In subtree of 3 no color appears exactly 8 times.
In subtree of 4 only color 2 appears exactly 1 time.
In subtree of 5 no color appears exactly 2 times.
In subtree of 6 only color 3 appears exactly 1 time.

In subtree of 1 colors 83, 97, 109, 121, 105, 115, 99, 104 occur exactly 1 time.
In subtree of 2 no color appears exactly 3 times.
In subtree of 3 colors 109 and 109 occur exactly 2 times.
In subtree of 4 no color appears exactly 5 times.
In subtree of 5 no color appears exactly 2 times.
In subtree of 6 no color appears exactly 2 times.
In subtree of 7 color 99 appears exactly 1 time.
In subtree of 8 no color appears exactly 2 times.
In subtree of 9 no color appears exactly 4 times.
In subtree of 10 color 97 appears exactly 1 time.
In subtree of 11 color 114 appears exactly 1 time.
In subtree of 12 color 109 appears exactly 1 time.
In subtree of 13 color 105 appears exactly 1 time.
In subtree of 14 color 110 appears exactly 1 time.
In subtree of 15 color 103 appears exactly 1 time.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a depth-first search (DFS) approach to traverse the tree and calculate the frequency of each color in the subtree of each vertex. We can use a hash map to store the frequency of each color. For each vertex, we can then check how many colors have a frequency equal to the lucky number of the vertex.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) — The time complexity is linear because we visit each vertex once during the DFS traversal.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — The space complexity is linear because in the worst case, we might need to store the frequency of each color for each vertex.
